response.justification: A seasoned generalist with a current Staff title, strong TypeScript, and genuinely relevant AI-agent tooling experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks), which matches the AI-native environment well. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from his skills and work history, his deepest high-throughput distributed-systems work (millions of requests/second at Sociomantic) dates to 2011-2013, and the resume shows little evidence of directing technical strategy, stakeholder coordination at scale, or operational/on-call ownership expected at Staff level.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience, directly matching the AI-native engineering baseline
Strong TypeScript/Node.js across recent product roles, plus polyglot depth (DLang, C++, Java) and a testing/craftsmanship mindset
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work handling millions of HTTP requests per second
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume, a stated must-have for the role
Large-scale distributed-systems experience is over a decade old; recent work skews full-stack product and tooling
Little demonstrated technical-strategy leadership, cross-functional stakeholder management, or incident-response/on-call ownership
